// Negative fixture for spec 120 R6 scan (g), round-11 DELTA
// (fail-closed exec-constructor aliasing): two shapes that previously
// escaped spawn discovery SILENTLY must now surface for classification.
//
//   1. LOCAL function-value alias of the exec seam:
//        spawn := exec.Command; spawn(program, id)
//      The direct-spawn Ident arm formerly consulted only package-level
//      exec seam vars, so a local `spawn` never entered discovery.
//   2. PACKAGE seam var bound to exec.CommandContext (cmdArgIdx 1):
//        var execCommandContext = exec.CommandContext
//      The seam-var matcher formerly matched only Sel.Name=="Command".
//   3. LOCAL `var spawn = exec.Command` (DeclStmt/ValueSpec):
//        var spawn = exec.Command; spawn("bd", "show", id)
//      analyzeFuncLocals/resolveLocalAlias formerly inspected only
//      AssignStmt, so the var-decl binding form escaped.
package fixture

import (
	"context"
	"os/exec"
)

// Package seam var over CommandContext — command operand is arg index 1.
var execCommandContext = exec.CommandContext

func aliasEscape(id string) error {
	program := "bd"      // single-assignment string literal -> "bd"
	spawn := exec.Command // single-assignment local exec alias
	return spawn(program, "show", id).Run()
}

func ctxSeamEscape(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
	return execCommandContext(ctx, "bd", "show", id).Run()
}

// The remaining local binding form: `var spawn = exec.Command` (a
// DeclStmt/ValueSpec, not an AssignStmt). Must also surface.
func localVarDeclAliasEscape(id string) error {
	var spawn = exec.Command // single-initialization local exec alias
	return spawn("bd", "show", id).Run()
}

// Tuple var-decl binding form: `var spawn, _ = exec.Command, 0` — the
// DeclStmt paths must handle tuple ValueSpecs pairwise, not single-only.
func tupleVarDeclAliasEscape(id string) error {
	var spawn, _ = exec.Command, 0
	return spawn("bd", "show", id).Run()
}
